cve.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in the way that the SMBv3 protocol handles certain requests, aka 'SMBGhost'.

A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Microsoft Windows when the Microsoft Server Message Block 1.0 (SMBv1) server receives a specially crafted request. An attacker who successfully exploited the vulnerability could execute arbitrary code on a targeted system. This vulnerability is similar to CVE-2017-0144, CVE-2017-0145, CVE-2017-0146, and CVE-2017-0148.

The SMB server in Microsoft Windows Vista, Windows Server 2008, and Windows Server 2008 R2 does not properly validate certain SMB requests, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted SMB request that triggers a buffer overflow.

A remote code execution vulnerability exists in the way that the Remote Desktop Gateway (RD Gateway) handles certain RD Gateway connections. An authenticated attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could execute arbitrary code on the RD Gateway server. To exploit this vulnerability, an attacker would have to send specially crafted requests to an RD Gateway server.

A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Remote Desktop Services - formerly known as Terminal Services - when an attacker connects to the target system and makes special requests through the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could gain access to the affected system. This vulnerability is also known as BlueKeep.

A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Remote Desktop Services (RDS) when an attacker connects to the target system and makes special requests through the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could gain access to the affected system.
